row090_contract.title: Visual contact inference, timing, force, and source-target ownership authority
row090_contract.completion_criteria: Actor-specific contact certification requires trusted source/target ownership and calibrated onset/peak/release evidence.
row090_contract.candidate_boundary: Candidate-only output is allowed when mask or contact authority is blocked, but certification is not.

contact_authority_boundary: Track source hand and target actor or body region with ownership.
Infer approach, contact, compression or recoil, release, velocity, clothing coverage, and visibility.
Align the transient to contact onset and rustle or settle envelopes to motion windows.
Block certification when source or target ownership or body or contact geometry is untrusted.
fail_closed_boundaries: Unknown source or target ownership permits candidate mixes only below certification ceiling.
Ambiguous onset permits multi-anchor candidate or windowed sync, never a frame-exact claim.

qa_protocol.timing_boundary: Frame-exact contact targets no more than half a source frame error, with stricter sample-domain thresholds where calibrated; evaluators record frame and sample errors.
qa_protocol.benchmark_boundary: Benchmarks include visible, clothed, occluded, and multi-actor hand or body contact, fixed and moving cameras, cuts, slow motion, variable frame rate, offscreen events, and intentionally silent motion.

legacy_wave22_contact_graph_partial_auth.compiler_behavior: Copies contact_edges from input and supplies default QA goal labels; it performs no visual inference.
legacy_wave22_contact_graph_partial_auth.validator_behavior: Requires only root keys, a nonempty edge list, and presence of eleven edge fields; it does not validate value types, nonempty identities, enumerations, ranges, frame spans, evidence hashes, or ownership provenance.
legacy_wave22_contact_graph_partial_auth.scorer_behavior: Coerces seven input values with bool(), then requires all coerced values true.

adversarial_probe.finding: Each JSON string value false was truthy under Python bool(), producing seven true checks and a false-positive PASS.

legacy_wave22_contact_graph_partial_auth.inventory_boundary: The current Main Flow has future contact-pass anchors, but the inventory explicitly says physical interaction runtime is not proved and contact evidence must not be inferred from prompt text or LoRA filenames.

visual_contact_manifest_requires: run, scene, shot, and take identity
contact edges with owner, entity, material, visual-force, frame-span, and expected-event fields
validated_input_contract.boundary: The evaluator consumes a pre-existing visual_contact_manifest. It does not generate contact edges from tracks, masks, landmarks, depth, or motion and does not infer peak or release anchors.

foley_evidence_current_authority.boundary: The downstream alignment stack is technically useful and fail-closed, but its own signed evidence proves that authoritative visual-contact, Wave22 force, event-linkage, run, shot, take, gold-mask, runtime, AV-review, and allowlist authority is absent.

wave31_force_routing_partial_authority.boundary: The router maps declared force_type, intensity, material, and times to an audio layer and sync precision. It does not infer contact, ownership, or calibrated timing from visual evidence.

shared_blockers: contact shadow is subtle or only subtle-to-moderate
placement remains shoulder or top-upper-sleeve instead of the exact target
static_hand_contact_visual_partial_autho.boundary: These are bounded static-image local-support reviews. They do not establish approach, onset, peak, release, duration, calibrated force, temporal visibility, or cross-frame ownership.

focused_test_result.scope: Downstream request construction, foley-force alignment validation, and declared clothing-contact QA only

repository_implementation_search.boundary: Compiler validates declared ownership/phase packets and projects consumer manifests; it does not yet extract contacts from tracked visual evidence under completed Rows085-089.

required_contact_inference_contract_miss: A strict input manifest binding video, frame clock, scene, shot, take, actor and object tracks, masks, landmarks, depth, motion, and material evidence by hash.
A production inference implementation that derives source owner, source body or object region, target owner, target body region or surface, and contact identity from visual evidence.
Approach, onset, peak compression or impact, release, end, and duration anchors with frame, seconds, sample, uncertainty, and source-frame provenance.
A calibrated force-band model using actor-relative motion, camera compensation, deformation or recoil, contact persistence, and uncertainty.
Visibility, occlusion, partial-view, and reappearance logic that preserves ownership and lowers authority when evidence is hidden or ambiguous.
A deterministic authority ceiling that emits candidate-only output when ownership, masks, geometry, force, or timing is untrusted.
A strict output schema with exact types, enums, ranges, unique event IDs, nonempty owner and region IDs, ordered anchors, provenance, confidence, and blocker fields.
A visual_contact_manifest producer rather than only a consumer-side validator.
Positive, negative, malformed, false-positive, multi-actor, occluded, moving-camera, cut, slow-motion, variable-frame-rate, repeated-contact, offscreen, and intentionally-silent tests.
Calibrated held-out benchmarks measuring ownership error, onset or peak or release error, force classification, false contacts, missed contacts, and authority-ceiling correctness.
Genuine runtime artifacts and combined frame, contact, and audio QA for the exact Row090 output.
Completed authority for all five dependency rows.

acceptance_matrix_required.acceptance: Rows085 through 089 each have row_complete=true with exact hash-bound authority.
Tracks, masks, landmarks, depth, motion, materials, clocks, and media are hash-bound to one scene, shot, and take.
The inferred source entity and region match trusted ownership under multi-actor and occlusion tests.
The inferred target entity and region or surface match trusted ownership under contact and reappearance tests.
Approach, onset, peak, release, and end anchors are ordered, uncertainty-bound, and calibrated in frame and sample domains.
Force bands are calibrated against relative motion, deformation or recoil, and held-out labels.
Blocked ownership or geometry can emit candidates but cannot certify frame-exact actor-specific contact.
Malformed types, missing fields, duplicate IDs, invalid spans, and string Boolean lookalikes fail closed.
Synthetic, adversarial, and genuine temporal benchmarks meet frozen ownership, timing, force, false-event, and missed-event thresholds.
Exact implementation hashes, test log, runtime record, contact overlays, audio alignment, combined review, and tracker output all pass.

scheduler_and_worker_containment.note: The audit does not re-enable or alter paused Codex automations, disabled AI-worker tasks, disabled Mask maintenance tasks, or the EC2 read-only safety monitor.

hold_decision.reason: A strict schema and fail-closed ownership/visual-contact compiler slice now exist with focused tests, and Wave22 declaration validators remain fail-closed. Rows085-089 remain incomplete, no CV contact inference from tracks/masks/landmarks/depth/motion is implemented, and no genuine runtime or combined frame/contact/audio review exists.
hold_decision.safe_next_work: Keep Row090 held. After Rows085-089 produce trusted track/mask/landmark/depth/motion/material packets, extend the compiler into a real visual inference producer with calibrated force and temporal benchmarks, then emit TRK-W64-090_contact_inference_ownership.json plus runtime and combined-review evidence.

row090_compiler_slice.scope: Introduces a production-oriented fail-closed compiler that validates ownership/phase/force/visibility packets, projects a consumer visual_contact_manifest, and blocks certification while Rows085-089 or runtime/review authority remain unsatisfied.

enforced_controls: exact Boolean dependency and runtime authority fields
dependency hold blocks decision=certified and non-candidate authority ceilings
ordered approach/onset/peak/release/end phase frames with seconds/sample conversion
null or untrusted ownership cannot certify
visual_contact_manifest projection forces production_trust_claim=false under hold
blocked unowned contacts are excluded from consumer edges
